title: Theory of Computation - Regular Language - Theoretical/Not Answered

questions.question:
Given the following two languages :

\( L_{1} = {uww^{R} ν | u, v, w \in (a, b)^{+}} \)

\( L_{2} = {uww^{R} ν | u, ν, w \in (a, b)^{+} , |u| \geq |ν|} \)

Which of the following is correct ?



UGC NET CSE | August 2016 | Part 3 | Question: 55
options: \( L_{1} \) is regular language and \( L_{2} \) is not regular language.
\( L_{1} \) is not regular language and \( L_{2} \) is regular language.
Both \( L_{1} \) and \( L_{2} \) are regular languages.
Both \( L_{1} \) and \( L_{2} \) are not regular languages.

questions.question:
Consider the following languages:

\( L_1=\{a^{\grave{z}^z} \mid \grave{Z} \text{is an integer} \} \)

\( L_2=\{a^{z\grave{z}} \mid \grave{Z} \geq 0\} \)

\( L_3=\{ \omega \omega \mid \omega \epsilon \{a,b\}^*\} \)

Which of the languages is(are) regular?

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:



UGC NET CSE | October 2020 | Part 2 | Question: 56
